rationale.fidelity: Explicitly diagnoses availability as an exogenous state and restores nonanticipative first-stage assignments plus observed, limited, scenario-feasible reassignment.
rationale.managerial_framing: Provides a concrete actor, trigger, two choices, cost stakes, hedge-and-recourse mechanism, trade-off, and no-recourse or alternate-policy counterfactual.
rationale.scholarly_positioning: No scholarly positioning is requested or claimed; this conditional category is not applicable.
rationale.evidence_discipline: Labels failure-scenario representativeness as an unvalidated model assumption and avoids performance claims beyond the model.
rationale.prose_clarity: Precise but substantially longer and more repetitive than needed to diagnose the state-as-decision error.
rationale.overall: A rigorous repair of both stages with no actual hard failure in the submitted answer; deductions concern economy and limited validation detail.
